properties.Name: Owl Saint George
properties.Author: birdO
properties.about: A towering, geometric owl that serves as a landmark for the Annex and a tribute to the neighborhood’s wisdom.
properties.Interesting Fact: The mural covers nearly the entire side of the hotel and was inspired by the "wisdom" of the Annex neighborhood.
properties.Designer Note: A masterclass in "large-scale grid design"; birdO uses high-contrast geometric blocks to create organic curves that are visible from blocks away.
properties.Clue / Hint: Find the wise bird watching Bloor!

properties.Name: Blanco Cantina Mural
properties.Author: Unknown (Retail Wrap)
properties.about: A high-impact digital vinyl wrap featuring vibrant, prismatic mariachi-themed art.
properties.Interesting Fact: This mural is actually a high-quality vinyl wrap, which sparked a local debate about the difference between hand-painted art and digital printing.
properties.Designer Note: The design uses vibrant "Day of the Dead" iconography to create a high-impact commercial brand touchpoint in a historic area.

properties.Name: Rise of the Pollinator
properties.Author: Nick Sweetman
properties.about: A massive mural highlighting urban biodiversity through macro-scale depictions of bees and flowers.
properties.Interesting Fact: At 65 feet wide, this mural features a giant bumblebee and flowers to raise awareness for urban biodiversity.
properties.Designer Note: Sweetman uses "macro-perspective" to turn a tiny insect into a monumental hero, forcing a change in the viewer's scale perception.
properties.Clue / Hint: Spot the giant bee near Howland!

properties.Name: City of the Future
properties.Author: Talie Shalmon
properties.about: A futuristic line-art vision of Toronto’s potential urban evolution in a century's time.
properties.Interesting Fact: This mural was commissioned to imagine what Toronto might look like in 100 years, featuring elevated transit and greenery.
properties.Designer Note: The "isometric" design style makes the flat wall feel like a three-dimensional blueprint or a scene from a classic video game.

properties.Name: Lee’s Palace
properties.Author: Al Runt (Alex Currie)
properties.about: Iconic club with a psychedelic facade that serves as a landmark for the Annex neighborhood.
properties.Interesting Fact: The mural has been repainted three times by the original artist to maintain its vibrant, "dancing monster" energy.
properties.Designer Note: A legendary example of "horror vacui" design—where every square inch of the surface is filled with detail to create a sense of chaos and energy.
properties.Clue / Hint: Monsters dancing on a purple wall!

properties.Name: Gallanough Block
properties.Author: Various artists (StART)
properties.about: A historic Edwardian commercial block in the Annex with a rhythmic and balanced industrial facade.
properties.Interesting Fact: This series of murals depicts the history of the Annex, including the "Spadina Expressway" protests that saved the neighborhood.
properties.Designer Note: Uses a "split-panel" layout similar to a graphic novel to guide the viewer through a chronological narrative of the city.

properties.Name: Barbara Barrett Lane
properties.Author: Elicser Elliott
properties.about: A lane-way mural project that brings hidden artistic life to the narrow alleys of the Annex.
properties.Interesting Fact: This lane is named after a local community advocate and features Elicser's signature "expressive characters."
properties.Designer Note: Elicser’s use of soft, blended spray-paint gradients creates a "human" feel that contrasts beautifully with the rough alley textures.
properties.Clue / Hint: Faces hidden in the quiet lane!

properties.Name: Annex Wall
properties.Author: Daniela Rocha
properties.about: A community-focused mural that captures the unique, eclectic spirit of the Annex neighborhood.
properties.Interesting Fact: The artist (Planta Muisca) incorporates Colombian-inspired patterns with Canadian flora to symbolize multicultural growth.
properties.Designer Note: The "organic flow" of the vines is designed to mimic the natural movement of pedestrians walking toward the subway station.

properties.Name: Massey Hall Mural
properties.Author: Various Artists
properties.about: A celebratory mural depicting legendary musicians who have shaped Toronto's iconic concert history.
properties.Interesting Fact: Created during the hall's massive renovation to celebrate the legendary musicians who have performed on its stage.
properties.Designer Note: The design uses "rhythmic composition"—the placement of the portraits mimics the peaks and valleys of a musical score.
properties.Clue / Hint: Spot the legends of the stage!

properties.Name: Old York Lane Mural
properties.Author: Various Artists
properties.about: High-fashion, chic murals tucked into a Yorkville alleyway as part of a public art project.
properties.Interesting Fact: Part of the "Yorkville Murals" festival, which aims to turn the luxury district into a public art playground.
properties.Designer Note: The "minimalist" color palette (lots of black, white, and gold) matches the high-end retail environment of the Village.
properties.Clue / Hint: Chic art in the narrow lane!

properties.Name: The Band of Storytellers (Blues Mural)
properties.Author: Adrian Hayles
properties.about: A massive, multi-storey tribute to the musical legends who defined the Yonge Street sound, from jazz to blues.
properties.Interesting Fact: The mural features legendary artists like Ronnie Hawkins and Glenn Gould, towering 22 storeys above the street.
properties.Designer Note: A study in 'monumental rhythm'; the verticality of the tower is used to stack the history of Toronto's music scene like a skyscraper of sound.
properties.Clue / Hint: Look for the giant blue faces of music legends!

properties.Name: 794 Bathurst
properties.Author: Various / Jarvis
properties.about: A vibrant, large-scale community mural that captures the eclectic, artistic spirit of the Annex and Mirvish Village.
properties.Interesting Fact: This wall has been a rotating canvas for years, often used to express the local struggle between heritage and development.
properties.Designer Note: An example of 'visual activism'; the colors and shapes are designed to reclaim the street corner from construction noise.
properties.Clue / Hint: Find the colorful abstract wall near the park!

properties.Name: History of Music on Yonge
properties.Author: Adrian Hayles
properties.about: The second half of the massive musical tribute, focusing on the jazz and R&B roots of the iconic 'Yonge Street Strip.'
properties.Interesting Fact: Hayles used archival photographs from the 1950s and 60s to ensure the portraits were historically accurate.
properties.Designer Note: The 'Blue Tone' creates a unified visual language that turns a generic brick building into a specialized historical archive.
properties.Clue / Hint: Spot the blue jazz legends on the corner!

properties.Name: Sassafraz Facade
properties.Author: Local Artists
properties.about: Rotating art and floral installations that refresh the historic yellow facade of a Yorkville landmark.
properties.Interesting Fact: While the building is a historic yellow house, the facade often features rotating art installations or intricate floral "wraps."
properties.Designer Note: A study in "adaptive reuse"; the art works with the Victorian architecture rather than covering it up, creating a "living" brand.
properties.Clue / Hint: Find the iconic little yellow house!
